ticket.subject: TNT residential or commercial shipping selection
ticket.raw_subject: TNT residential or commercial shipping selection
ticket.description:
I'm not getting acturate quoting on TNT because there is no selection of either commercial or residential for the customer to choose, TNT have told me this : Hi Jason,

 

I understand what you are asking for, and it is something our API does, it can include residential surcharges. The problem lay with pluginhive, if they haven’t included the option in their plugin then we can’t do much for you unfortunately. I would suggest reaching out for support from Pluginhive, if they wish to add the option get them to contact me and I can work through the requirements with them for it.

 

If I can assist anymore, please let me know.

 

Kind Regards,

 

Nick Evans | Customer Solutions Specialist | FedEx Express / TNT Australasia | t: 0466 835 497

 

48 Monash Road Redbank, QLD 4301, Australia

 I understand what you are asking for, and it is something our API does, it can include residential surcharges. The problem lay with pluginhive, if they haven’t included the option in their plugin then we can’t do much for you unfortunately. comments.body:
🤖 Auto-generated draft — probation mode (not sent to customer)
Product: shopify-mcsl | Signal: form_field | Confidence: HIGH
─────────────────────────────────────────────

Hello Jason,

Thank you for reaching out and for sharing the details from your conversation with TNT — that context is very helpful.

You are correct that the MCSL app does not currently include a residential vs. commercial address type selector for TNT. This means the app is unable to request residential surcharges from TNT's API at the time of rate calculation, which leads to the underquoting you are experiencing for residential deliveries.

As an interim workaround, you can add a blanket rate adjustment to your TNT rule to account for the likely residential surcharge on applicable orders. To do this:

1. Go to App Settings → Shipping Rates → Rate Automation
2. Edit your TNT Australia rule
3. Under Action Details, select "Adjust Shipment Price" and enter a percentage or flat amount that approximates the residential surcharge
4. Save the rule

We understand this is not ideal — a flat adjustment applies to all orders regardless of whether the destination is residential or commercial, so it is a workaround rather than a proper solution.

We will pass your feedback along with the TNT contact details you have shared to our product team as a feature request. We appreciate Nick Evans' offer to work through the API requirements with us — that information will be included when we raise this internally.

We are unable to commit to a timeline for when this feature would be available, but your report and the direct TNT confirmation that the API supports it are valuable input.

If the rate adjustment workaround helps bridge the gap in the meantime, please let us know. If you have any other questions, feel free to reach out.

Thanks & Regards,
Team PluginHive

⚡ This response was generated automatically by PluginHive AI Support. A team member will follow up if you need further assistance.

comments.body:
Hi Jason,

Thank you for reaching out and for sharing the information from TNT.

Could you please include (CC) your TNT Australia point of contact in the email thread? This will allow us to discuss the API capabilities directly with them and review the implementation requirements with our development team.

We value your feedback and would like to make any necessary changes or improvements, where feasible, to provide you with the best possible experience using our app.
Once we have the relevant details, we'll be happy to evaluate the requirements and provide you with an update.

We look forward to hearing from you.

Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

To connect with Nick directly about the TNT API requirements, could you please share his email address as well? It was not visible in the portion of the email pasted into this thread. We'll keep this ticket open and update you once we have more information from the TNT team on the requirements.

Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hi Nicholas,

I hope you are doing well.

My name is Priyanka, and I am from the PH Multi Carrier Shipping Label app by PluginHive for Shopify.

I am reaching out on behalf of our mutual customer who is using our solution for TNT Australia integration (Account Number: 50010959).

The customer reached out to us mentioning that they would like residential surcharges (if applicable) to be included in the shipping costs shown to their customers at checkout.
They were also advised by the TNT team that the residential surcharge may not currently be part of the shipment request being sent. We would like to better understand how residential surcharges are supported through the TNT API and whether any additional parameters or changes are required in our integration to ensure these charges are returned in the rate response.

I have attached the relevant API logs for your reference. Could you please review them and let us know:
• Whether residential surcharges are available through the API responses.
• Any specific parameters that need to be passed in the rate request.
• Any API documentation or implementation guidelines that would help us support this functionality.

We would greatly appreciate any guidance you can provide.

Thank you for your time and assistance. We look forward to hearing from you.

Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:

I am reaching out on behalf of our mutual customer who is using our solution for TNT Australia integration (Account Number: 50010959).
 
The customer reached out to us mentioning that they would like residential surcharges (if applicable) to be included in the shipping costs shown to their customers at checkout.
They were also advised by the TNT team that the residential surcharge may not currently be part of the shipment request being sent. We would like to better understand how residential surcharges are supported through the TNT API and whether any additional parameters or changes are required in our integration to ensure these charges are returned in the rate response.
 
I have attached the relevant API logs for your reference. Could you please review them and let us know:
• Whether residential surcharges are available through the API responses.
• Any specific parameters that need to be passed in the rate request.
• Any API documentation or implementation guidelines that would help us support this functionality.
 
We would greatly appreciate any guidance you can provide.
 
Thank you for your time and assistance. We look forward to hearing from you.
 
Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hi Team,



Residential surcharges are simple to implement; they require one line of code per receiver and sender address. The address is not validated so if you are including them in your platform, I suggest you create a toggle box for the customers to say whether it is residential.



I have attached the RTT documentation to this email it shows the inclusion of the Residential surcharge, you’ll be looking for the lines:

<residential>RSD</residential> after the delivery or receiver and <residential>RSP</residential> for collection or sender.



RSD= Delivery

RSP = Pickup



Have a look at the documentation and I am happy to advise as needed. I also modified one of the request examples you send me you’ll be able to see the changes it isn’t much to add.

Kind Regards,



**Nick Evans** | Customer Solutions Specialist | FedEx Express / TNT Australasia | t: 0466 835 497



48 Monash Road Redbank, QLD 4301, Australia
comments.html_body:
Hi Team,

 

Residential surcharges are simple to implement; they require one line of code per receiver and sender address. The address is not validated so if you are including them in your platform, I suggest you create a toggle box for the customers to say whether it is residential.

 

I have attached the RTT documentation to this email it shows the inclusion of the Residential surcharge, you’ll be looking for the lines:

<residential>RSD</residential> after the delivery or receiver and <residential>RSP</residential> for collection or sender.

 

RSD= Delivery

RSP = Pickup

 

Have a look at the documentation and I am happy to advise as needed. I also modified one of the request examples you send me you’ll be able to see the changes it isn’t much to add.

Kind Regards,

 

Nick Evans | Customer Solutions Specialist | FedEx Express / TNT Australasia | t: 0466 835 497

comments.body:
L3:
https://pluginhive.slack.com/archives/C02E15X8X0C/p1781669361793909

Request – TNT Australia Residential Surcharge Support

Customer Requirement:
MCSL customer using TNT Australia would like residential surcharges (if applicable) to be included in the shipping rates displayed at checkout.

We contacted the TNT Australia team regarding the implementation of residential surcharges through their API.
TNT confirmed that residential surcharges can be implemented by including the following tags in the API request:
"<residential>RSD</residential> after the delivery or receiver and <residential>RSP</residential> for collection or sender.
RSD= Delivery
RSP = Pickup"

Please review the TNT documentation and assess the feasibility of implementing support for residential surcharge in the TNT Australia integration.

Kindly check the attachment below.

Please review the TNT documentation and assess the feasibility of implementing support for residential surcharge in the TNT Australia integration.
 
Kindly check the attachment below.

Hello Nick and Jason,

Thank you for your patience.

We have reviewed the residential surcharge requirement with our development team and confirmed that we will be enhancing the app to support residential surcharges for TNT Australia rates.
Our team is currently assessing the implementation effort and will provide an estimated timeline (ETA) by tomorrow.

If any questions come up during development, we may reach out for further guidance — and we appreciate your offer to assist.

Thanks and regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

Hello Jason,

The shipping rate for Chile is failing with the message that the recipient's postal code or suburb/city information is invalid.
Could you please try placing the order again using a valid Chilean shipping address and verify whether the rates are returned successfully?

Please let us know how it goes. If you need any assistance, we'll be happy to help.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Had a call with the customer and informed them that the rates are failing because the total girth exceeds the carrier's limit of 140 inches.

The customer mentioned that they recently updated the product dimensions, which is likely causing the issue. They will revert the dimension changes and perform further testing.

The customer appreciated the quick call and assistance provided.
